user_outcome: A release operator and every later Baton release-mode adapter can consume Sworn's portable board-oracle-v1 projection, derive only safe topology facts, and fail closed before ambiguous ownership or unsafe ref selection causes worktree or Git action.
rationale: The v0.16 portable board oracle replaces assumptions about engine-specific worktree fields with a deliberately small public projection. Sworn already has ref-aware discovery evidence, but it needs one schema-backed projection validator and public reachability contract. This slice owns that boundary only; it does not implement future role, merge, or replan command behavior. It processes committed technical metadata, logs no secrets, and uses deterministic linear traversal plus sorted set checks.
in_scope: Emit and validate aggregate board-oracle-v1 catalogs with release, safe sourceRef, nested ordered tracks, and nested ordered slice rows containing id, state, track, and dependsOnTracks.
Preserve the explicitly named-release compatibility JSON shape containing only release and tracks; it intentionally omits sourceRef.

Normalize null dependency and empty-track compatibility spellings before validation, then reject duplicate track IDs, duplicate slice ownership, row/parent track mismatch, inconsistent normalized dependency sets within a track, and aggregate map-key/release mismatch before a consumer derives a path, tests a dependency, or selects a Git object.

Treat sourceRef as an empty read-only filesystem fallback sentinel or a syntactically safe fully qualified refs/heads or refs/remotes name. Provide a reusable mutating-consumer guard that rejects the empty sentinel and verifies the exact ref before object selection.

Retain ref-aware release-wt topology selection and branch-accurate status election, but never require or serialize optional worktree path, worktree branch, blocked-by, ready-to-merge, or track lifecycle convenience fields as portable authority.
out_of_scope: Vendoring board-oracle-v1 bytes and installer commands belongs to S23-v016-parity-and-installs.
Implementing role lifecycle, merge, replan, or shipped transitions belongs to S14 through S17; those slices consume C-19 after it verifies.
Changing persisted board-v1 plan semantics, release requirements, or user-facing TUI design is not part of this projection-boundary slice.
Provider calls, credentials, model configuration, proxy routing, and any public/private commercial content are prohibited.

effort_complexity.rationale: The contract spans compatible JSON emission, cross-node semantic validation, safe Git identity, and an existing ref-aware oracle without broadening authority.

risks.description: A consumer accepts a structurally valid-looking but ambiguously owned projection and acts on the wrong track.
risks.mitigation: Centralize normalization and integrity validation, test every forbidden topology, and make consumers require its result before deriving paths or refs.

risks.description: A filesystem fallback or revision expression becomes a mutable Git selector.
risks.mitigation: Accept only schema-safe fully qualified refs for mutating use, verify them exactly with Git, and reject the empty sentinel.

risks.description: Compatibility support silently starts depending on optional Sworn-specific fields.
risks.mitigation: Test output with those fields absent and derive branch, worktree, dependencies, and merge state from naming and Git only.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the built sworn binary runs board --json without a release filter THE SYSTEM SHALL emit a board-oracle-v1 aggregate catalog whose selected release entries contain release, sourceRef, and nested tracks/slices, and whose slice rows contain id, state, track, and dependsOnTracks; IF emission or schema validation fails THEN the command SHALL exit non-zero without partial JSON (N-16).

WHEN the built sworn binary runs board --release <release> --json THE SYSTEM SHALL retain exactly the compatibility top-level release and tracks shape without sourceRef, normalize null dependencies and null empty-track spelling for validation, and preserve nested ordered rows (N-16).

IF an aggregate projection has duplicate track IDs, duplicate slice IDs, a row whose track differs from its enclosing track, inconsistent normalized dependsOnTracks within one track, or a release-map key that differs from entry.release THEN the shared validator and every board command boundary using it SHALL reject the projection before deriving a worktree path, dependency, or Git object (N-16).

WHEN a release-mode caller requests mutable use of an aggregate sourceRef THE SYSTEM SHALL accept only a schema-safe fully qualified refs/heads or refs/remotes name that Git verifies exactly; IF sourceRef is empty, malformed, a revision expression, or missing then it SHALL fail before object selection or mutation (N-16).

WHILE deriving release-mode topology THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT require or treat optional worktreePath, worktreeBranch, blockedBy, readyToMerge, or track state fields as authority; branch/worktree identity, dependencies, and merged state remain derived from the locked naming convention and Git (N-16).
